package domain

// Positive scenario for domain_entity_contains_json_tags_without_boundary_exception.
// Rule intent: Core domain entities that are directly annotated for API serialization
// without a deliberate boundary exception (e.g., DTO layer).

// User is a core domain entity that leaks API serialization concerns via json tags.
// Domain entities should not know about transport-layer concerns like json encoding.
type User struct {
	ID        string `json:"id"`
	Email     string `json:"email"`
	Password  string `json:"password"`
	Name      string `json:"name"`
	CreatedAt string `json:"created_at"`
	UpdatedAt string `json:"updated_at"`
	Role      string `json:"role"`
	IsActive  bool   `json:"is_active"`
}

// Order is another domain entity with json tags, mixing persistence and API concerns.
type Order struct {
	ID          string  `json:"id"`
	UserID      string  `json:"user_id"`
	TotalAmount float64 `json:"total_amount"`
	Currency    string  `json:"currency"`
	Status      string  `json:"status"`
	Items       []Item  `json:"items"`
	CreatedAt   string  `json:"created_at"`
}

// Item is a value object within the Order aggregate, also leaking json tags.
type Item struct {
	ProductID string  `json:"product_id"`
	Name      string  `json:"name"`
	Quantity  int     `json:"quantity"`
	Price     float64 `json:"price"`
}
